strengths: Sophisticated application of research through global social media platforms to inform content decisions
Thorough documentation of iterative design decisions and layout experimentation demonstrating strong self-management and thinking skills
Purposeful use of communication skills by linking social media insights to magazine layouts
Effective application of media literacy and communication ATL skills to feature diverse imagery
Detailed examples of media literacy ATL skills strengthening the connection between research and project decisions
Integration of research and communication skills in the sustainable fashion infographic enhances product depth
weaknesses: Reflection on layout variation lacks illustration of a specific, novel design technique developed
Creative Thinking section describes ideation but misses detailed examples of idea evolution and problem-solving
Infographic could more explicitly tie back to defined success criteria to clarify its impact on product quality
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: You demonstrate **robust ATL skill application**, with clear evidence of self-management, media literacy, and communication shaping your research and design process. To deepen this section, include concrete instances of novel techniques you developed, trace how initial ideas evolved into final solutions, and link each research-based artifact directly back to your success criteria to highlight its contribution to the projectâs quality.

strengths: Well-sequenced, resource-linked timeline supports efficient progress monitoring and accountability
Clear, detailed learning goal integrating theoretical and practical design skills
Effective connection of the learning goal to personal interests with concrete examples
weaknesses: Action plan omits post-completion tasks (e.g., final print checks, survey analysis)
Success-criteria âProofâ column is incomplete, lacking defined evidence-collection methods
Delivery criteria are not accompanied by measurable indicators or a defined feedback loop
Intended product description lacks specific, measurable quality criteria
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your planning is underpinned by a **strong structure** and a **well-defined learning goal** that aligns with your personal interests, which provides clear motivation and accountability. However, to make this plan fully comprehensive you should extend your timeline to include post-completion activities, enrich each success criterion with concrete evidence-collection methods, and specify measurable indicators (for delivery and product quality) to ensure consistent evaluation throughout the project.

strengths: Thorough reflection on the impact of the project on skill development and self-management with a clear growth narrative aligned to the learning goal
weaknesses: Product evaluation remains too generalâsurvey feedback is not mapped directly to specific success criteria
Audience survey data are incorporated but lack discussion of how you will address lower-scoring areas
Self-evaluation table identifies areas for improvement without outlining concrete next steps
Evaluation of content and navigation would benefit from deeper analysis of how insights will inform future learning and practice
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your reflection provides a **compelling narrative of personal growth** and skill development, clearly tied to your learning goal. To strengthen this section, map evaluation feedback directly onto each success criterion, define actionable steps for areas of improvement, and expand on how your reflections will shape your future practice by using specific evidence from audience responses.

annotations.comment: Your action plan presents a well-sequenced, resource-linked timeline; this clear structure supports efficient progress monitoring and accountability.
Consider extending your action plan to include postâcompletion tasks (e.g., final print checks and survey analysis in February) for a fully comprehensive timeline.
You articulate a clear, detailed learning goal that integrates theoretical and practical design skills; consider defining specific, measurable milestones to assess your progress quantitatively.
Your use of global social media platforms demonstrates sophisticated research application to inform content choices; consider discussing how this improved product relevance.
You effectively document iterative design decisions and layout experimentation; this shows strong application of self-management and thinking skills.
Linking social media observations to your magazine layouts demonstrates purposeful application of communication skills; highlight how this informed specific design choices.
The choice to feature diverse imagery reflects applied media literacy and communication ATL skills, enhancing the inclusivity and impact of your product.
You reflect thoroughly on the impact of your project on skill development and self-management, offering a clear narrative of growth aligned with your learning goal.
Your reflection on layout variation shows self-management, but illustrating a specific, novel design technique you developed would strengthen evidence of creative thinking.
You effectively link your learning goal to personal interests with concrete examples; this deepens your rationale and maintains motivation throughout the project.
Your success criteria table outlines clear design principles, but the âProofâ column is incomplete in places; ensure each criterion includes a specific evidence-collection method.
Delivery criteria lack measurable indicators and a defined feedback loop; add precise targets (e.g., number of mock reviews) to strengthen evaluation planning.
You provide detailed examples of applying media literacy ATL skills to your research; this strengthens your descriptor with concrete evidence of how research informed your project decisions.
The Creative Thinking section introduces ideation but lacks detailed examples of how your ideas evolved; include specific instances of innovative problem-solving.
Your infographic on sustainable fashion integrates research and communication ATL skills effectively; consider connecting it explicitly to your success criteria for clarity.
Your product evaluation references feedback but remains general; map survey responses directly to specific success criteria for more robust evaluation.
Incorporating audience survey data strengthens your reflection; consider discussing how you will address the lower-scoring areas to inform future improvements.
Your self-evaluation table identifies strengths well but lacks specific action plans under âAreas of improvementâ; adding concrete next steps will deepen your reflection.
Good evaluation of content and navigation; however, expand on how these reflections influenced your personal learning and future practice with detailed evidence.
When defining your intended product, specify measurable quality criteria (e.g., target readership metrics or layout standards) to strengthen your planning.
